author: Gopal Shastri, P.
title: Jiwan Charitr Gopal Sharma Shastri ka aur ati Gopal Vallabha Ratan Sangrah
gloss: Select shalokas or Sanskrit couplets, with a brief autobiography of the compiler.
notes: Sanskrit couplets bundled with the compiler's Hindi autobiography.

title: Sandhia
gloss: The daily prayer of the Hindus.
notes: Reg 1130 — among the earliest regs of the quarter. P. Roda Ram again (see Q4 Punjabi—Poetry s260, s264–265).

flags.issue: printed serial reads 6 after 4 — either 5 is a misprint here or serial 5 appeared out of our range; transcribed as printed

author: Deva Ram, Lama
title: Kalchakr, or a calendar for 1911
publisher: The author, who is a Lama, of Lapta, Bashahar State
notes: Only TIBETAN section in our 1910 extractions — a Lama from Bashahr hill state printing a Kalachakra calendar at Lahore; the catalog's classificatory reach at its geographic edge. Format 'Oblong' (pothi-style).

author: Tulsi Das, Mehta
title: Rag Binod
gloss: A pamphlet containing instructions in singing and playing musical instruments.
notes: Rag Binod — Urdu manual for singing and instrumental raga practice; music pedagogy entering the print market (cf. project interest in musical knowledge circulation). Reg 1406 = highest reg seen so far this quarter.

author: Karamat-ullah, Mir
title: Rafiq-i-Azam
gloss: A brief sketch of the life of Imam-i-Azam, the founder of the Hanafi sect of Muhammadans. He lived about a hundred years after Muhammad.
notes: Urdu—Biography serials continue from Q3 (ended s39). Anjuman free-distribution tract on Abu Hanifa.

printer_verbatim: Anand Prakash and Mujaddidi Presses, Amritsar.
author: Kishan Singh, Bhai
title: Jang-nama
gloss: A brief account of the life of Banda, the famous Sikh leader who fought against the Muhammadan rulers in the Punjab in the time of the successors of Aurangzeb.
notes: Banda Bahadur biography in Urdu, split across two presses — one Hindu-named (Anand Prakash), one Naqshbandi (Mujaddidi) — printing a narrative of Sikh–Mughal war.

author: Muhammad Insha-ullah, Maulvi
title: Imam-i-Ghazali
gloss: A brief biographical sketch of the life of Imam Ghazali, a celebrated Muhammadan philosopher and theologian, born in 450 Hijri.
notes: Page count printed with damaged digit ('Pp. ?6'); read as 16 given 32° pamphlet pricing at 2 annas.

flags.issue: first digit of page count unclear; read as 16
